mnemonics: Static Stays: Static members belong to the class itself, not to any specific instance. Think 'Shared Single' for class-wide storage.
keyPoints: Static members are associated with the class, not with individual objects.
Static methods can only directly call other static methods and access static variables.
A static block is used for static initialization of a class and executes when the class is loaded.
commonMistakes: Trying to access instance variables or methods directly from a static method (causes a compiler error).
Assuming that a static variable holds a unique value for each object instance (it is shared by all instances).
